titleStmt.title: Letter from M.L. Waller to Sergeant Thomas McGrath, 12 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: M.L. Waller

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from M.L. Waller to Sergeant Thomas McGrath (c.1864-1919). Waller writes of the proposed exemption from internment or the release of an unspecified 'alien enemy,' possibly a German citizen living in Ireland.Waller was the head of the Prisoner of War Division at the Home Office. McGrath was serving as chief constable of the Dublin Metropolitan Police at the time

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/6514/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Any communication on the
subject of this letter should be
addressed to: -
THE UNDER SECRETARY OF STATE,
HOME OFFICE,
LONDON, S.W.,
and the following number quoted :
P.W. 304361 HOME OFFICE, WHITEHALL. 12.4.16 Sir, I am directed by the Secretary of State to transmit
to you the accompanying papers, and to say that he will
be obliged if you will kindly report on the enclosed form
your opinion on the question of the exemption from
internment or release of the alien enemy therein referred
to. It will be convenient if the envelope containing your
reply is marked on the outside with the words ''Prisoners
of War'.' Please return the enclosures with your reply. I am,
Sir,
your obedient Servant, M. L Waller Chief Cons
Horsepad
Tues.
13/4/16 Text below the date is unclear Supt. A. Dum Sergeant McGrath,
FarCreharts
LWaller/ a Draft
The Chief Constable. 14/4/16
City Police Force
Dublin
